name: Room Connection Graph
description: A generated graph that defines how rooms, arenas, corridors, or level nodes connect.
design_notes: Room connection graphs define pacing before geometry exists. They should encode start, exit, critical path, branches, locks, rewards, loops, and validation rules so generated levels remain navigable and purposeful.
design_purpose: Control pacing through critical path length and branch rewards.
Support keys, shops, bosses, secrets, and optional rooms.
player_fantasy: The player feels they are exploring a coherent space with choices, loops, and goals.

parameters.name: critical_path_node_count
parameters.description: Rooms required between start and exit.

parameters.name: branch_probability
parameters.description: Chance that a graph node creates an optional branch.

parameters.name: loop_connection_budget
parameters.description: Extra edges used to create loops or shortcuts.

parameters.name: required_room_tag_ids
parameters.description: Room types that must appear in a valid graph.

parameters.name: graph_validation_retries
parameters.description: Attempts to rebuild a graph that fails constraints.

edge_cases: Lock-and-key graphs must prove keys appear before locks on reachable paths.
Secret rooms should not block required progression.
Room templates can fail to connect even when graph edges are valid.
common_bugs: Graph validation checks connectivity but not critical path ordering.
Random branch generation creates unreachable isolated components.
Seed changes between graph and room placement make debugging impossible.
balancing_notes: Long critical paths increase run commitment; branch density increases exploration and reward choice.
Loops improve navigation but can reduce tension if every route bypasses risk.
accessibility_notes: Generated layouts should support readable maps or landmarks when graph complexity is high.

implementation_notes.unity: Generate the abstract room graph before geometry, validate start-to-exit reachability and lock/key ordering, then instantiate prefabs from node metadata.
implementation_notes.godot: Represent graph nodes and edges as dictionaries or Resources, run connectivity and required-room validation, then place room scenes after the graph passes constraints.
implementation_notes.unreal: Build graph data in a generator actor or subsystem, validate critical-path and branch constraints, then spawn level instances or room actors from node definitions.
implementation_notes.web: Generate graph nodes and edges as plain data with a stored seed, validate reachability and room tags, then feed the graph into tile, canvas, or WebGL placement.

example_games.title: Slay the Spire
example_games.note: Uses a connected node map to structure run choices between encounters.

example_games.title: The Binding of Isaac
example_games.note: Uses connected rooms to structure each floor layout.
